item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: The second image that was used in this article is non-compliant with the SCP wiki's license (CC BY-SA 3.0) as no source indicates it has been released under Creative Commons. Thus, it has been removed. The first image comes from here and is cc-compliant.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Those beacons, those are actually ant colonies cast with aluminium, aren't they? I like it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
reminds me of powder game on dan-ball.jp
those ants can go through anything, man.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This needs a lot of writing cleanup. Grammatical errors abound and the SCP-2349-1 designation is redundantly explained in two different paragraphs. The writing feels disorganized and much more stream-of-consciousness than a professional report. The three paragraphs preceding (regarding the D-Class test) the first addendum would work better as an experiment log or some such thing. The last sentence of the first of those three paragraphs in particular is awkwardly worded. Simply stating that the ants of the second colony moved to impede the D-Class without the bit about direction and where he was coming from would convey the same message more clearly. As it is, there isn't any clear transition from "this is the thing and this is what it does" and "also this thing happened."

Right now, the writing makes it a downvote from me. Cleaning it up and making it more professional would at least make it a neutral vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Core concept is good. Execution is serviceable, IMO. Where'd you find the photo of the ants' radio antenna?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I agree. The core idea is very nice but it requires some cleanup.
+1 regardless

(as I said with 2031, "Artificial Intelligence? More like Ant Intelligence! amiright?) :D

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The initial SCP-2349 colony was found in Singapore. Reports of electrical failures, metal disappearances, and unusual radio signals prompted Foundation investigation.

Just to let you know such things did indeed happen in my country a while back (minus the radio signals part), so props to you for tying it into reality. That said, I agree with others that this needs to be improved. Perhaps a clarification on their intelligence level (are these sentient or sapient? Given that they can broadcast human language I'm leaning on the latter) and whether these are of terrestrial or alien origin could make it better.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm still rather unimpressed with the whole thing. At the end of the day, these are a bunch of metallo-ants who might be aliens or something? It's not very strongly developed, and as I said in the drafting phase, very little is done with this idea.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: As OP ants go, this is good. The relay antenna (antenna, hah!) and transmission at the end elevate this beyond other articles that have done similar things. It needs some writing cleanup, however.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Like this. Ants were always one of my weak spots.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'll be the odd man out and say that I actually liked this one. The idea of a group of techno-ants searching the planet for their missing queen is pretty interesting, and I was left wondering who created these things (if anyone). More wouldn't hurt, flesh it out and whatnot, but I do like it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This has a good concept but is a little open and vague for me to enjoy it. It could use some tidying up but besides that I think it's pretty good so hope you keep working on it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like the idea, but I feel like it's a little all over the place. There's also a lot of tone issues, too. Towards the bulk of the description, the writing begins to sound like a science class lab write up. That can all be fixed easily though.I think as long as you brush up the writing and make the direction clear, this could become a solid SCP!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The eggs solution is fine, but I still don't buy them being able to break such a short message, even if it is in a human language.

Setting that aside, however, this still isn't very interesting. We have some other techno-ants, and the story here is unclearly presented and not with a very strong direction. So these are cyborg ants that are maybe alien drones? We don't end very far from where we start, but you spend a lot of words taking us there.

I am pleased, however, that you're working to improve this. It's good to adapt work to feedback, even if that feedback comes after posting.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The footnote has been added.

item.title: Rewrote reproduction and translation
item.content:encoded: Listening to the complaints that people had, I tweaked the issues that have been brought up with the SCP. While they might not fix it, I tried to remedy it. While one of these was vocalized on the other was not when I had asked, sorry for any confusion it may have caused.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The core idea is solid, in my oppinion.
I would like more in the reproduction angle, the fact they simply won't reproduce if observed seems like a cheap way out… and for sure the translation was a bad move since it gives away too much while making no sense. They hapened to trasmit in binary, in english? And this wasn't noticed before, or those happened to use a different system than all the others which makes even less sense since to learn the language they should be in an area where it's used, so Argentina wouldn't be a good candidate.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah, I think that would work.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Perhaps a footnote would be an easy and effective method of avoiding further confusion?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The core concept isn't bad, but the implementation is problematic. I don't like how they stop reproducing under careful inspection, which just feels like a cop out. I also don't buy the Foundation being able to translate the broadcast. With so little to go on, how could they possibly decipher a language with presumably no connection to human ones?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I figured that out, however, I just feel that while reading a description on something, I shouldn't have to make an assumption about something that large. Just my opinion, though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Uh, the castes being used are the actual ant ones. Workers, Warriors, Nurses, Queens, etc.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like the idea of insects building radio towers, but with the tonal issues, and vague references to castes without further elaboration, I can't upvote.
